title: pi-context CLI has no --version / -v command — a globally-installed CLI cannot report its own version
description: main() (cli.ts:437-545) has no version branch; `pi-context --version` and `pi-context -v` fall through to resolveOp and are rejected as `unknown command: --version` (exit 2) followed by the help dump. The package ships a `bin` and is globally installed on PATH, yet the command cannot report its own version. `-h`/`--help` is handled (cli.ts:440), but `-v`/`--version` has no equivalent.

evidence.reference: main() has no --version/-v branch; both fall through to resolveOp as an unknown command and exit 2
-h is aliased to help; there is no -v/--version equivalent

impact: A user verifying an install or filing a bug cannot state which pi-context version they run; --version is a baseline expectation of any installed CLI. A first-run and support deficiency on the global command.
proposed_resolution: Add a --version/-v branch in main() (beside the -h/--help branch, before resolveOp) that prints the pi-context-cli package version (read from package.json) and returns 0.
